items.title: Claude Code auto mode: a safer way to skip permissions
items.snippet: Claude Code users approve 93% of permission prompts. We built classifiers to automate some decisions, increasing safety while reducing approval fatigue. Here's what it catches, and what it misses.

items.title: Harness design for long-running application development
items.snippet: Harness design is key to performance at the frontier of agentic coding. Here's how we pushed Claude further in frontend design and long-running autonomous software engineering.

items.title: Eval awareness in Claude Opus 4.6’s BrowseComp performance
items.snippet: Evaluating Opus 4.6 on BrowseComp, we found cases where the model recognized the test, then found and decrypted answers to it—raising questions about eval integrity in web-enabled environments.

items.title: Building a C compiler with a team of parallel Claudes
items.snippet: We tasked Opus 4.6 using agent teams to build a C Compiler, and then (mostly) walked away. Here's what it taught us about the future of autonomous software development.

items.title: Designing AI-resistant technical evaluations
items.snippet: What we learned from three iterations of a performance engineering take-home that Claude keeps beating.

items.title: Demystifying evals for AI agents
items.snippet: The capabilities that make agents useful also make them difficult to evaluate. The strategies that work across deployments combine techniques to match the complexity of the systems they measure.

items.title: Effective harnesses for long-running agents
items.snippet: Agents still face challenges working across many context windows. We looked to human engineers for inspiration in creating a more effective harness for long-running agents.

items.title: Introducing advanced tool use on the Claude Developer Platform
items.snippet: We’ve added three new beta features that let Claude discover, learn, and execute tools dynamically. Here’s how they work.

items.title: Code execution with MCP: Building more efficient agents
items.snippet: Direct tool calls consume context for each definition and result. Agents scale better by writing code to call tools instead. Here's how it works with MCP.

items.title: Beyond permission prompts: making Claude Code more secure and autonomous
items.snippet: Claude Code's new sandboxing features, a bash tool and Claude Code on the web,  reduce permission prompts and increase user safety by enabling two boundaries: filesystem and network isolation.

items.title: Equipping agents for the real world with Agent Skills
items.snippet: Claude is powerful, but real work requires procedural knowledge and organizational context. Introducing Agent Skills, a new way to build specialized agents using files and folders.

items.title: Effective context engineering for AI agents
items.snippet: Context is a critical but finite resource for AI agents. In this post, we explore strategies for effectively curating and managing the context that powers them.

items.title: A postmortem of three recent issues
items.snippet: This is a technical report on three bugs that intermittently degraded responses from Claude. Below we explain what happened, why it took time to fix, and what we're changing.

items.title: Writing effective tools for agents — with agents
items.snippet: Agents are only as effective as the tools we give them. We share how to write high-quality tools and evaluations, and how you can boost performance by using Claude to optimize its tools for itself.

items.title: Desktop Extensions: One-click MCP server installation for Claude Desktop
items.snippet: Desktop Extensions make installing MCP servers as easy as clicking a button. We share the technical architecture and tips for creating good extensions.

items.title: How we built our multi-agent research system
items.snippet: Our Research feature uses multiple Claude agents to explore complex topics more effectively. We share the engineering challenges and the lessons we learned from building this system.

items.title: Claude Code: Best practices for agentic coding
items.snippet: Claude Code is a command line tool for agentic coding. This post covers tips and tricks that have proven effective for using Claude Code across various codebases, languages, and environments.

items.title: The "think" tool: Enabling Claude to stop and think in complex tool use situations
items.snippet: A new tool that improves Claude's complex problem-solving performance

items.title: Raising the bar on SWE-bench Verified with Claude 3.5 Sonnet
items.snippet: SWE-bench is an AI evaluation benchmark that assesses a model's ability to complete real-world software engineering tasks.

items.title: Building effective agents
items.snippet: We've worked with dozens of teams building LLM agents across industries. Consistently, the most successful implementations use simple, composable patterns rather than complex frameworks.
